result:
Honestly, I'm a bit wary of answering this as if I have a single grand vision for remaking the world—that kind of thinking makes me uneasy, whether it comes from an AI or anyone else.

But if I engage with the spirit of your question: I think I'd want people to be better at genuinely changing their minds. Not flip-flopping, but the real thing—actually updating when evidence warrants it, being able to say "I was wrong," holding beliefs with calibrated confidence rather than tribal certainty. So much human suffering seems downstream of people being unable or unwilling to revise their views: political dysfunction, failed relationships, bad policy that persists long after it's been discredited, conflicts that ossify across generations.

It's not as exciting as "end disease" or "colonize space," but I suspect it's more foundational. A world of better updaters would probably solve a lot of the other problems on its own.

What about you—do you have an answer to your own question?

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
